target.name: Cerebras
target.founder: Andrew Feldman, Gary Lauterbach, Michael James, Sean Lie, Jean-Philippe Fricker

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cerebras is a public-stage semiconductor company founded by Andrew Feldman and four co-founders. The company has raised $1.1B+ to date and currently carries a $23B valuation. The applicable peer universe comprises 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables: Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation approach. The median valuation across the 3 public semiconductor peers is $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. The public-comp anchoring method using sector P/S multiples (20.6x median across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied, as Cerebras' revenue is not on file and private companies rarely disclose this metric publicly.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, implies a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median point estimate of $1.5B. This substantially undercuts the current $23B market valuation. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set for a company with disclosed differentiation in AI chip architecture, the absence of revenue-based cross-checks, and the wide dispersion within the peer group ($1.0B to $5.0B). A revenue multiple validation and deeper peer selection refinement would strengthen conviction in any range derived here.
